Wise and good people may make mistakes, which they will correct as soon as they realize them. David does not try to excuse what was done wrong, nor to blame others; but he admits that he and others were guilty of not seeking God in the proper way.

It is helpful to recognize Divine Providence’s assistance, even in things within our natural abilities; if God did not help us, we could not move. If we perform our religious duties correctly, even a little, we must acknowledge that God helped us; if we had been left to ourselves, we would have made serious errors. And everything we do must be done depending on the mercy of God through the sacrifice of the Redeemer.
